---
title: "Figure 5"
author: "Phil Zhou"
date: "2025-11-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r load_packages}
library(svglite)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)
library(gplots)
library(cowplot)
library(zoo)
library(patchwork)
library(corrplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(rstatix)
library(here)
```

```{r load_dfs}
BEAN_FUSE_FL <- read_csv(
  here("data", "0326_LDLR_mastersheet.csv")
)

domains <- read_tsv(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "LDLR_domains.tsv")
)

LDLR_GOF <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "LDLR_GOF_validation_final_8_16_25.xlsx")
)

LDLR_LOF <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "LDLR_LOF_validation_final_8_16_25.xlsx")
)

LDLR_GOF_LOF <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "LDLR_GOF+LOF_final_8_16_25.xlsx")
)
```

```{r fig_5A}
# ---- 1) Explicitly rename columns ----
domains <- domains %>%
  rename(
    start  = `Domain AA start`,
    end    = `Domain AA end`,
    domain = `LDLR domain (Uniprot)`
  )

# ---- 2) Collapse to one value per residue ----
#Note that we are plotting the per-residue FUSE score average instead of the FUSE score for every variant. 
#Here we take the mean over each variant sharing a residue position. 
df_residue <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  select(Position, score = `LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`) %>%
  drop_na(Position, score) %>%
  group_by(Position) %>%
  summarize(score = mean(score), .groups = "drop")

# ---- 3) Assign variants to structural domains ----
df_long <- df_residue %>%
  tidyr::crossing(domains) %>% #Generates all possible combinations of (residue, domain)
  filter(Position >= start, Position <= end) %>% #Keep only combinations where residues fall within domain boundaries
  select(Position, score, domain)

# ---- 4) Define interaction residues ----
#We have pre-specified positions where LDLR interacts with the proteins MYLIP (E3 ubiquitin ligase) and PCSK9.
#Because these residues do not cleanly fit into a contiguous domain, we select them in a separate step. 

MYLIP_positions <- c(813, 816, 817, 820, 821, 823)

PCSK9_positions <- c(
  295, 297, 298, 299, 300, 301,
  306, 307, 308, 309, 310, 312,
  318, 355, 396, 626
)

# ---- 5) Add MYLIP/PCSK9-interacting regions ----
df_interactions <- df_residue %>%
  filter(Position %in% c(MYLIP_positions, PCSK9_positions)) %>%
  mutate(
    domain = case_when(
      Position %in% MYLIP_positions ~ "MYLIP-interacting",
      Position %in% PCSK9_positions ~ "PCSK9-interacting"
    )
  )

# Combine structural + interaction annotations
df_long <- bind_rows(df_long, df_interactions)

# ---- 6) Set domain order for labels on the x-axis of the final figure ----
domain_cols <- c(
  unique(domains$domain),
  "MYLIP-interacting",
  "PCSK9-interacting"
)

df_long <- df_long %>%
  mutate(domain = factor(domain, levels = domain_cols))

# ---- 7) Pretty labels ----
pretty_labels <- domain_cols

pretty_labels <- gsub("^\\s+|\\s+$", "", pretty_labels)
pretty_labels <- gsub("\\.+", " ", pretty_labels)

pretty_labels <- sub("^(LDLR class A) (\\d+)$", "\\1 repeat \\2", pretty_labels)
pretty_labels <- sub("^(LDLR class B) (\\d+)$", "\\1 repeat \\2", pretty_labels)
pretty_labels <- sub("^(EGF-like) (\\d+)$", "\\1 repeat \\2", pretty_labels)

pretty_labels <- sub("^Ca2\\+ binding$", "Ca²⁺ binding region", pretty_labels)
pretty_labels <- sub("^Disordered$", "Disordered region", pretty_labels)

# turn into named vector for ggplot
pretty_labels <- setNames(pretty_labels, domain_cols)

# ---- 8) Plot ----
fig_5A <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = domain, y = score, fill = domain)) +
  geom_violin(scale = "width", trim = TRUE, color = "black") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.12, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, alpha = 0.3, size = 0.6) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "LDLR-FL FUSE score") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = scales::hue_pal()(length(domain_cols))) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = pretty_labels) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 20, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_5A

# ---- 9) Save ----
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_5"), "Fig5A.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_5A,
  width    = 8,
  height   = 10,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_5B}
# ---- 1) Map each residue to a domain ----
df_mapped <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  select(Position, z = `LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(domains) %>%
  filter(Position >= start, Position <= end) %>%
  select(Position, z, domain)

# ---- 2) Compute per-domain stats ----
# We are specifically subsetting variants to either z < -1.96 or z > 1.96 
df_summary <- df_mapped %>%
  group_by(domain) %>%
  summarise(
    total_residues = n(),
    n_decreased = sum(z < -1.96, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_increased = sum(z > 1.96, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_decreased = n_decreased / total_residues,
    prop_increased = n_increased / total_residues
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 3) Order domains (biological order) ----
domain_order <- domains$domain

df_summary <- df_summary %>%
  mutate(domain = factor(domain, levels = domain_order)) %>%
  arrange(domain)

# ---- 4) Build matrix for heatmap ----
data <- rbind(
  df_summary$prop_decreased,
  df_summary$prop_increased
)

colnames(data) <- df_summary$domain
rownames(data) <- c(
  "# significantly decreased uptake",
  "# significantly increased uptake"
)

# ---- 5) Clean column labels (same logic as before) ----
cn <- colnames(data)
cn <- gsub("^\\s+|\\s+$", "", cn)
cn <- gsub("\\.+", " ", cn)

labCol <- cn
labCol <- sub("^(LDLR class A) (\\d+)$", "\\1 repeat \\2", labCol)
labCol <- sub("^(LDLR class B) (\\d+)$", "\\1 repeat \\2", labCol)
labCol <- sub("^(EGF-like) (\\d+)$", "\\1 repeat \\2", labCol)
labCol <- sub("^Ca2\\+ binding$", "Ca²⁺ binding region", labCol)
labCol <- sub("^Disordered$", "Disordered region", labCol)

# ---- 6) Color palette ----
pal <- viridis::viridis(256, option = "D")

# ---- 7) Plot heatmap ----
fig_5B <- gplots::heatmap.2(
  data,
  scale = "none",
  col = pal,
  trace = "none",
  density.info = "none",
  dendrogram = "none",
  Rowv = FALSE, Colv = FALSE,
  labCol = labCol,
  key = TRUE, key.title = "Value", key.xlab = "Proportion",
  margins = c(16, 16),
  srtCol = 90,
  cexCol = 0.85,
  adjCol = c(1, 1),
  cexRow = 1.0
)

# ---- 8) Save ----
pdf(file.path(here("output", "figure_5"), "Fig5B.pdf"), width = 12.8, height = 7.19)
gplots::heatmap.2(
  data,
  scale = "none",
  col = pal,
  trace = "none",
  density.info = "none",
  dendrogram = "none",
  Rowv = FALSE, Colv = FALSE,
  labCol = labCol,
  key = TRUE, key.title = "Value", key.xlab = "Proportion",
  margins = c(16, 16),
  srtCol = 90,
  cexCol = 0.85,
  adjCol = c(1, 1),
  cexRow = 1.0
)
dev.off()
```


```{r fig_5C}
label_size <- 6

# ---- 1) Highlighting MYLIP/PCSK9 interacting residues ----
mylip_res <- c(813, 816, 817, 823)
pcsk9_res <- c(316, 318, 319, 320, 321, 322, 327, 328, 329, 330, 331, 333, 339, 376, 417, 647)

# ---- 2) GOF variants (data frame) ----
GOF_targets <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  filter(`Variant ID` %in% c("G219D", "S213L", "K223E", "K223N")) %>%
  group_by(`Variant ID`) %>%
  slice_max(`LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(lbl = `Variant ID`)

# ---- 3) LOF variants ----
LOF_targets <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  filter(`Variant ID` %in% c("D221Y","D224H")) %>%
  group_by(`Variant ID`) %>%
  slice_max(`LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(lbl = `Variant ID`)

# ---- 4) Define residue domain regions and color ----
BEAN_FUSE_FL <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  mutate(domain = case_when(
    Position >= 25  & Position <= 313 ~ "LDLR class A",
    Position >= 314 & Position <= 393 ~ "EGF-like",
    Position >= 397 & Position <= 658 ~ "LDLR class B",
    Position >= 663 & Position <= 712 ~ "EGF-like",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ),
  domain_color = case_when(
    domain == "LDLR class A" ~ "#60C3D1",
    domain == "EGF-like"     ~ "#C3C45E",
    domain == "LDLR class B" ~ "#CF4D44",
    TRUE                     ~ "#BCBCBC"
  ))

# ---- 5) Highlight PCSK9/MYLIP-interacting residues ----
BEAN_FUSE_FL <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  mutate(
    special_color = case_when(
      Position %in% mylip_res ~ "#E0A159",  # yellow/orange
      Position %in% pcsk9_res ~ "#A65BDB",  # purple
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    plot_color = dplyr::coalesce(special_color, domain_color)  # prefer special if present
  )

# ---- 6) Compute rolling mean per residue position ----
# Note that the mean is a rolling average with a window size of 5 as specified by the parameter k in rollmean
LDLR_avg <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  filter(!is.na(`LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`)) %>%
  group_by(Position) %>%
  summarise(mean_mu = mean(`LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Position) %>%
  mutate(roll_mu = rollmean(mean_mu, k = 5, fill = NA, align = "center"))

# ---- 7) Plot ----
fig_5C <- ggplot(BEAN_FUSE_FL, aes(Position, `LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = plot_color), size = 2, alpha = 0.7, shape = 16, stroke = 0) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1.96, 1.96),
             linetype = "dotted",
             color = "black",
             linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "LDLR residue position", y = "LDLR-FL BEAN z score") +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 18)
  ) +

  #Highlight GOF/LOF variants
  geom_point(data = GOF_targets, aes(`Position`, `LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`),
             shape = 1, size = 3, stroke = 1.2, color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = GOF_targets, aes(Position, `LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`, label = `Variant ID`),
                           size = label_size, inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  geom_point(data = LOF_targets, aes(`Position`, `LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`),
             shape = 1, size = 3, stroke = 1.2, color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(data = LOF_targets, aes(`Position`, `LDLR-FL BEAN_score normalized`, label = `Variant ID`),
                           size = label_size, inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  #Domain boundaries + rolling mean
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(195, 233), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_line(data = LDLR_avg, aes(x = Position, y = roll_mu), color = "black", linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.7)

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_5C

# ---- 8) Save ----
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_5"), "Fig5C.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_5C,
  width    = 10,
  height   = 5,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_5D}
# Load data
combined_df <- LDLR_GOF

# ---- 1) Detect the construct column ----
construct_candidates <- c(
  "Construct type","Construct name","Construct",
  "construct_type","construct_name","construct"
)
construct_col <- intersect(construct_candidates, names(combined_df))
if (length(construct_col) == 0) {
  construct_col <- names(combined_df)[grepl("(?i)^construct", names(combined_df))]
}
stopifnot(length(construct_col) >= 1)
construct_col <- construct_col[1]

# ---- 2) Detect replicate columns like "073025 rep 1" using regex ----
rep_cols <- grep("^\\d{6}\\s*rep\\s*\\d+$", names(combined_df),
                 value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE)
stopifnot(length(rep_cols) >= 1)

# ---- 3) Normalize construct labels ----
combined_df <- combined_df %>%
  mutate(
    !!construct_col := .data[[construct_col]] %>%
      as.character() %>%
      str_replace_all("\\u00A0", " ") %>%
      str_squish() %>%
      str_replace(regex("^wt$", ignore_case = TRUE), "WT")
  )

# ---- 4) Desired full ordering ----
final_levels <- c(
  "WT",
  "D221Y",
  "D224H",
  "S213L",
  "G219D",
  "K223E",
  "S213L G219D",
  "S213L K223E",
  "G219D K223E",
  "S213L G219D K223E"
)

#We determined that these constructs were not worth plotting in the final figure, because they have variants besides the 
#three GOF (S213L, G219D, K223E) and two LOF (D221Y, D224H)
drop_constructs <- c(
  "S213L G219D K223E K225G",
  "H203K L205G S213L G219D K223E K225G",
  "H203K L205G S213L",
  "S213L G219D K223E K225G",
  "H203K L205G S213L G219D K223E K225G",
  "H203K L205G S213L"
)

keep_levels <- setdiff(final_levels, drop_constructs)

# ---- 5) Classify Singles vs Combinations ----
classify_group <- function(x) { 
  ifelse(
    x == "WT",
    "Singles",
    ifelse(str_detect(x, " "), "Combinations", "Singles")
  )
}

# ---- 6) Long format ----
long_all <- combined_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(rep_cols),
    names_to = "replicate",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    group = classify_group(.data[[construct_col]])
  )

# ---- 7) Summary statistics ----
sum_all <- long_all %>%
  group_by(.data[[construct_col]], group) %>%
  summarise(
    n        = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean_LFC = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_LFC   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem_LFC  = sd_LFC / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- 8) Define which constructs go into each figure panel ----

#Panel 1 contains individual variant LDL uptake, Panel 2 contains LDL uptake for combinations of GOF variants

#Individual variants (GOF+LOF)
levels_singles <- c("WT","D221Y","D224H","S213L","G219D","K223E")

#Combinations of GOF variants
levels_combos <- c(
  "WT",
  "S213L G219D",
  "S213L K223E",
  "G219D K223E",
  "S213L G219D K223E"
)

# Panel 1
sum_s1 <- sum_all %>%
  filter(.data[[construct_col]] %in% levels_singles) %>%
  mutate(!!construct_col := factor(.data[[construct_col]], levels = levels_singles))

long_s1 <- long_all %>%
  filter(.data[[construct_col]] %in% levels_singles) %>%
  mutate(!!construct_col := factor(.data[[construct_col]], levels = levels_singles))

# Panel 2
sum_s2 <- sum_all %>%
  filter(.data[[construct_col]] %in% levels_combos) %>%
  mutate(!!construct_col := factor(.data[[construct_col]], levels = levels_combos))

long_s2 <- long_all %>%
  filter(.data[[construct_col]] %in% levels_combos) %>%
  mutate(!!construct_col := factor(.data[[construct_col]], levels = levels_combos))

# ---- 9) Shared y-limits ----
ymin <- min(
  long_s1$value, long_s2$value,
  sum_s1$mean_LFC - sum_s1$sem_LFC,
  sum_s2$mean_LFC - sum_s2$sem_LFC,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
ymax <- max(
  long_s1$value, long_s2$value,
  sum_s1$mean_LFC + sum_s1$sem_LFC,
  sum_s2$mean_LFC + sum_s2$sem_LFC,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
pad <- 0.08 * (ymax - ymin)
y_limits <- c(ymin, ymax + pad)

# ---- 10) Helper plot function ----
make_one <- function(sum_df, long_df, y_limits, construct_col) {
  ggplot(sum_df, aes(x = .data[[construct_col]], y = mean_LFC)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = .data[[construct_col]]),
             color = "black", width = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "WT"                = "steelblue",
        "D221Y"             = "#DE647D",
        "D224H"             = "#DE647D",
        "S213L"             = "steelblue",
        "G219D"             = "steelblue",
        "K223E"             = "steelblue",
        "S213L G219D"       = "steelblue",
        "S213L K223E"       = "steelblue",
        "G219D K223E"       = "steelblue",
        "S213L G219D K223E" = "steelblue"
      ),
      guide = "none"
    ) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_LFC - sem_LFC,
                      ymax = mean_LFC + sem_LFC),
                  width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(
      data = long_df,
      aes(x = .data[[construct_col]], y = value),
      width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.75,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.6)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits,
                       expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02,0))) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean BODIPY-LDL ΔLFC") +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16, colour = "black"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black",
                                  margin = margin(r = 8)),
      axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.4, colour = "black"),
      axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "black"),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.margin = margin(6, 12, 24, 12)
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off")
}

# ---- 11) Build the figure panels ----
p1 <- make_one(sum_s1, long_s1, y_limits, construct_col)
p2 <- make_one(sum_s2, long_s2, y_limits, construct_col)

fig_5D <- p1+p2

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_5D

# ---- 12) Save ----
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_5"), "Fig5D.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_5D,
  width    = 10,
  height   = 5,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_5D t test}
# Make temporary copies with a simple column name
long_s1_test <- long_s1 %>%
  rename(construct = all_of(construct_col))

long_s2_test <- long_s2 %>%
  rename(construct = all_of(construct_col))

# Panel 1 (Singles)
stat_s1 <- compare_means(
  value ~ construct,
  data = long_s1_test,
  method = "t.test",
  method.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
  ref.group = "WT",
  p.adjust.method = "holm"
) %>%
  filter(group2 %in% c("D221Y", "D224H", "S213L", "G219D", "K223E")) %>%
  mutate(panel = "Singles")

# Panel 2 (Combinations)
stat_s2 <- compare_means(
  value ~ construct,
  data = long_s2_test,
  method = "t.test",
  method.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
  ref.group = "WT",
  p.adjust.method = "holm"
) %>%
  filter(group2 %in% c(
    "S213L G219D",
    "S213L K223E",
    "G219D K223E",
    "S213L G219D K223E"
  )) %>%
  mutate(panel = "Combinations")

# Combine + adjusted stars

stat_all <- bind_rows(stat_s1, stat_s2) %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif.adj = case_when(
      p.adj <= 1e-4 ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 1e-3 ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 1e-2 ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "n.s."
    )
  )

stat_all
```

```{r fig_5E}
#Figure 5E

df <- LDLR_LOF

# ---- 1) Detect columns ----
construct_candidates <- c("Construct type","Construct name","Construct",
                          "construct_type","construct_name","construct")
construct_col <- intersect(construct_candidates, names(df))
if (length(construct_col) == 0) {
  construct_col <- names(df)[grepl("(?i)^construct", names(df))]
}
if (length(construct_col) == 0) {
  stop("Couldn't find a construct column. Columns available:\n",
       paste(names(df), collapse=", "))
}
construct_col <- construct_col[1]

# Replicates like "073025 rep 1", "061825 rep 2", etc.
rep_cols <- names(df)[grepl("^\\d{6}\\s*rep\\s*\\d+$", names(df), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(rep_cols) == 0) {
  rep_cols <- names(df)[grepl("rep\\s*\\d+$", names(df), ignore.case = TRUE)]
}
if (length(rep_cols) == 0) {
  stop("Couldn't find replicate columns (e.g., '073025 rep 1'). Columns:\n",
       paste(names(df), collapse=", "))
}

message("Using construct column: ", construct_col)
message("Replicate columns: ", paste(rep_cols, collapse = ", "))

# ---- 2) Clean construct labels ----
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    !!construct_col := .data[[construct_col]] %>%
      as.character() %>%
      str_replace_all("\\u00A0", " ") %>%  # NBSP -> space
      str_squish() %>%
      str_replace(regex("^wt$", ignore_case = TRUE), "WT")
  )

# ---- 3) Long format ----
tmp_rep <- as.data.frame(df[, rep_cols, drop = FALSE], check.names = FALSE)
st      <- utils::stack(tmp_rep)    # columns stacked; gives `values` and `ind`
row_id  <- rep(seq_len(nrow(tmp_rep)), times = ncol(tmp_rep))
meta    <- df[, setdiff(names(df), rep_cols), drop = FALSE]

long_df <- cbind(
  replicate = as.character(st$ind),
  value     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st$values)),
  meta[row_id, , drop = FALSE]
)
long_df <- as.data.frame(long_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
long_df$replicate <- factor(long_df$replicate, levels = rep_cols)

# keep valid construct rows
long_df <- long_df %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[construct_col]]), .data[[construct_col]] != "")

# ---- 4) Summarise per construct ----
summary_df <- long_df %>%
  group_by(.data[[construct_col]]) %>%
  summarise(
    n        = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean_LFC = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_LFC   = sd(value,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sem_LFC  = sd_LFC / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # remove specific constructs
  filter(!.data[[construct_col]] %in% c("W87G", "D90N"))

# also remove them from the replicate-level data
long_df <- long_df %>%
  filter(!.data[[construct_col]] %in% c("W87G", "D90N"))

# ---- 5) Order: WT first then by descending mean ----
has_wt <- any(summary_df[[construct_col]] == "WT", na.rm = TRUE)
if (has_wt) {
  ordered_levels <- c(
    "WT",
    summary_df %>%
      filter(.data[[construct_col]] != "WT") %>%
      arrange(desc(mean_LFC)) %>%
      pull(!!rlang::sym(construct_col))
  )
} else {
  ordered_levels <- summary_df %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_LFC)) %>%
    pull(!!rlang::sym(construct_col))
}

summary_df <- summary_df %>%
  mutate(!!construct_col := factor(.data[[construct_col]], levels = ordered_levels))

long_df <- long_df %>%
  mutate(!!construct_col := factor(.data[[construct_col]], levels = ordered_levels))

# --- 6) Plot ---
fig_5E <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = .data[[construct_col]], y = mean_LFC)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#DE647D", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_LFC - sem_LFC, ymax = mean_LFC + sem_LFC),
                width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(
    data = long_df,
    aes(x = .data[[construct_col]], y = value),
    width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.75, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(na.translate = FALSE, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean BODIPY-LDL ΔLFC"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16, colour = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, colour = "black", margin = margin(r = 8)), # <- bigger y title
    axis.ticks   = element_line(linewidth = 0.4, colour = "black"),
    axis.line    = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "black")
  )

fig_5E

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_5"), "Fig5E.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_5E,
  width    = 5,
  height   = 5,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_5E t test}
# Rename construct column for easier testing
long_df_test <- long_df %>%
  rename(construct = all_of(construct_col))

# Keep only the constructs involved in the hypothesis family
keep_constructs <- c("WT", "E101K", "E240K", "D221G", "D227E")

long_df_test_sub <- long_df_test %>%
  filter(construct %in% keep_constructs)

# Run only WT-vs-variant comparisons, with Holm correction across those 4 tests
stat_5E <- compare_means(
  value ~ construct,
  data = long_df_test_sub,
  method = "t.test",
  method.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),   # Welch
  ref.group = "WT",
  p.adjust.method = "holm"
) %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif.adj = case_when(
      p.adj <= 1e-4 ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 1e-3 ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 1e-2 ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "n.s."
    )
  )

stat_5E
```

```{r fig_5F-I}
#Figure 5F-I

df <- LDLR_GOF_LOF

# ---- 1) Detect columns ----
construct_candidates <- c("Construct type","Construct name","Construct",
                          "construct_type","construct_name","construct")
construct_col <- intersect(construct_candidates, names(df))
if (length(construct_col) == 0) {
  construct_col <- names(df)[grepl("(?i)^construct", names(df))]
}
stopifnot("No construct column found" = length(construct_col) >= 1)
construct_col <- construct_col[1]

# Replicate columns like "073025 rep 1", "061825 rep 2", etc.
rep_cols <- names(df)[grepl("^\\d{6}\\s*rep\\s*\\d+$", names(df), ignore.case = TRUE)]
if (length(rep_cols) == 0) {
  rep_cols <- names(df)[grepl("rep\\s*\\d+$", names(df), ignore.case = TRUE)]
}
stopifnot("No replicate columns detected (e.g. '073025 rep 1')" = length(rep_cols) >= 1)

# ---- 2) Clean construct labels ----
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    !!construct_col := .data[[construct_col]] %>%
      as.character() %>%
      str_replace_all("\\u00A0", " ") %>%   # non-breaking space -> space
      str_squish() %>%
      str_replace_all("[[:space:]]+", "_") %>%  # spaces -> underscores (uniform)
      str_replace(regex("^wt$", ignore_case = TRUE), "WT")
  )

# ---- 3) Long format ----
tmp_rep <- as.data.frame(df[, rep_cols, drop = FALSE], check.names = FALSE)
st      <- utils::stack(tmp_rep)              # values + source column in `ind`
row_id  <- rep(seq_len(nrow(tmp_rep)), times = ncol(tmp_rep))
meta    <- df[, setdiff(names(df), rep_cols), drop = FALSE]

long_df <- cbind(
  replicate = as.character(st$ind),
  value     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st$values)),
  meta[row_id, , drop = FALSE]
)
long_df <- as.data.frame(long_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
long_df$replicate <- factor(long_df$replicate, levels = rep_cols)
long_df <- long_df %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[construct_col]]), .data[[construct_col]] != "")

# ---- 4) Helper (color bars per panel) ----
make_plot <- function(summary_sub, long_sub, title_text, y_limits = NULL,
                      red_label, red_hex = "#DE647D", purple_hex = "#A65BDB",
                      wt_hex = "steelblue") {

  summary_sub <- summary_sub %>%
    mutate(bar_color = dplyr::case_when(
      .data[[construct_col]] == "WT"      ~ wt_hex,
      .data[[construct_col]] == red_label ~ red_hex,
      TRUE                                ~ purple_hex
    ))

  p <- ggplot(summary_sub, aes(x = .data[[construct_col]], y = mean_LFC)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = bar_color), color = "black", width = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_LFC - sem_LFC, ymax = mean_LFC + sem_LFC),
                  width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_jitter(
      data = long_sub,
      aes(x = .data[[construct_col]], y = value),
      width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.75, inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(
  na.translate = FALSE,
  labels = function(x) gsub("_", " ", x)
    ) + 
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Mean BODIPY-LDL ΔLFC"
      # title = title_text
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12, colour = "black"),
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 12, colour = "black"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, colour = "black"),
      axis.ticks   = element_line(linewidth = 0.4, colour = "black"),
      axis.line    = element_line(linewidth = 0.5, colour = "black"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )

  if (!is.null(y_limits)) p <- p + coord_cartesian(ylim = y_limits)
  p
}

# ---- 5) Define the four panels (underscore style) ----
levels_E101K <- c("WT","E101K","G219D_E101K","S213L_E101K","S213L_G219D_K223E_E101K")
levels_D221G <- c("WT","D221G","G219D_D221G","S213L_G219D_D221G")
levels_D227E <- c("WT","D227E","G219D_D227E","S213L_G219D_D227E")
levels_E240K <- c("WT","E240K","G219D_E240K","S213L_E240K","S213L_G219D_K223E_E240K")

# ---- 6) Build data + share a common y-axis across all four ----
# Drop S213L_E101K from the E101K panel
dat_E101K <- summarize_for_levels(setdiff(levels_E101K, "S213L_E101K"))
dat_D221G <- summarize_for_levels(levels_D221G)
dat_D227E <- summarize_for_levels(levels_D227E)
# Drop S213L_E240K from the E240K panel
dat_E240K <- summarize_for_levels(setdiff(levels_E240K, "S213L_E240K"))

ymin <- min(
  dat_E101K$summary$mean_LFC - dat_E101K$summary$sem_LFC,
  dat_D221G$summary$mean_LFC - dat_D221G$summary$sem_LFC,
  dat_D227E$summary$mean_LFC - dat_D227E$summary$sem_LFC,
  dat_E240K$summary$mean_LFC - dat_E240K$summary$sem_LFC,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
ymax <- max(
  dat_E101K$summary$mean_LFC + dat_E101K$summary$sem_LFC,
  dat_D221G$summary$mean_LFC + dat_D221G$summary$sem_LFC,
  dat_D227E$summary$mean_LFC + dat_D227E$summary$sem_LFC,
  dat_E240K$summary$mean_LFC + dat_E240K$summary$sem_LFC,
  na.rm = TRUE
)
y_limits <- c(ymin, ymax)

# ---- 7) Build panels with requested colors ----
# Panel order left→right: E101K, E240K, D221G, D227E
# Red bar in each: the single LOF (E101K / E240K / D221G / D227E)
# All non-WT others: purple
p_E101K <- make_plot(dat_E101K$summary, dat_E101K$long,
                     "LDLR+/LDLR− FITC ΔLFC: +E101K", y_limits,
                     red_label = "E101K")

p_E240K <- make_plot(dat_E240K$summary, dat_E240K$long,
                     "LDLR+/LDLR− FITC ΔLFC: +E240K", y_limits,
                     red_label = "E240K")

p_D221G <- make_plot(dat_D221G$summary, dat_D221G$long,
                     "LDLR+/LDLR− FITC ΔLFC: +D221G", y_limits,
                     red_label = "D221G")

p_D227E <- make_plot(dat_D227E$summary, dat_D227E$long,
                     "LDLR+/LDLR− FITC ΔLFC: +D227E", y_limits,
                     red_label = "D227E")

# Make a single row of the four plots
fig_5F_I <- p_E101K | p_E240K | p_D221G | p_D227E

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_5F_I  

# ---- 8) Save ----
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_5"), "Fig5F-I.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_5F_I,
  width    = 15,
  height   = 5,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_5F_I statistical testing}
# ---- 1) Helper: safe Welch t-test ----

run_welch <- function(data, group_col, g1, g2) {

  sub <- data %>%
    filter(.data[[group_col]] %in% c(g1, g2)) %>%
    mutate(.group = droplevels(factor(.data[[group_col]]))) %>%
    filter(!is.na(value))

  # Safety check (this is what was causing your error)
  if (length(unique(sub$.group)) != 2) {
    return(tibble(
      group1 = g1,
      group2 = g2,
      n1 = NA,
      n2 = NA,
      mean1 = NA,
      mean2 = NA,
      p = NA
    ))
  }

  test <- t.test(value ~ .group, data = sub, var.equal = FALSE)

  tibble(
    group1 = g1,
    group2 = g2,
    n1 = sum(sub$.group == g1),
    n2 = sum(sub$.group == g2),
    mean1 = mean(sub$value[sub$.group == g1], na.rm = TRUE),
    mean2 = mean(sub$value[sub$.group == g2], na.rm = TRUE),
    p = test$p.value
  )
}

# ---- 2) Define comparisons ----

comparisons_by_panel <- list(
  Panel_1 = list(
    c("E101K", "G219D_E101K"),
    c("E101K", "S213L_G219D_K223E_E101K")
  ),
  Panel_2 = list(
    c("E240K", "G219D_E240K"),
    c("E240K", "S213L_G219D_K223E_E240K")
  ),
  Panel_3 = list(
    c("D221G", "G219D_D221G"),
    c("D221G", "S213L_G219D_D221G")
  ),
  Panel_4 = list(
    c("D227E", "G219D_D227E"),
    c("D227E", "S213L_G219D_D227E")
  )
)

# ---- 3) Run all tests ----

stat_results <- imap_dfr(comparisons_by_panel, function(comp_list, panel_name) {

  map_dfr(comp_list, function(pair) {
    run_welch(long_df, construct_col, pair[1], pair[2])
  }) %>%
    mutate(panel = panel_name)
})

# ---- 4) Holm correction (within panel) ----

stat_results <- stat_results %>%
  group_by(panel) %>%
  mutate(p.adj = p.adjust(p, method = "holm")) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 5) Add significance labels ----

stat_results <- stat_results %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif = case_when(
      is.na(p.adj)        ~ NA_character_,
      p.adj <= 0.0001     ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 0.001      ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 0.01       ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05       ~ "*",
      TRUE                ~ "ns"
    ),
    comparison = paste(group1, "vs.", group2)
  )

# ---- 6) Final clean output ----

stat_results %>%
  select(panel, comparison, n1, n2, mean1, mean2, p, p.adj, p.signif)
```
